Home/Epistemology

Branch Guide

Epistemology

Questions about belief, evidence, certainty, updating, and the tools we use to justify claims.

  • Branch Epistemology
  • Page form Branch guide
  • Best for regulating belief, evidence, confidence, and revision
  • Difficulty Foundational to intermediate

Why This Branch Matters

belief, justification, uncertainty, and the standards by which claims become responsible.

Epistemology matters because it gives the archive a standard for responsible confidence. It asks how perception, testimony, inference, memory, and probability should constrain belief.

The best route is to track how evidence changes credence, how justification differs from psychological comfort, and how skepticism can discipline thought without paralyzing it.

Core Tensions

The branch becomes useful when these pressures stay visible.

  • Belief and knowledge
  • Evidence and justification
  • Credence and updating
  • Skepticism without paralysis
